steps.title: Identify the central paradox
steps.content: The word However signals a critical shift in the passage: the bird's specialization is portrayed first as beneficial (it aids survival in its habitat) but then as a liability (it makes the bird especially vulnerable to environmental changes). The main idea must capture this trade-off, not just one side of it.

steps.title: Apply the paradox to the choices
steps.content: C directly expresses both sides of the trade-off: the bird is highly specialized and therefore particularly susceptible to habitat disturbances, with therefore making clear that vulnerability flows from specialization. A contradicts the passage on habitat sharing, B ignores the vulnerability entirely, and D inverts the causality. That matches choice C — C is correct.
